During the season, they competed in the [[Super League XXV]] and the [[2020 Challenge Cup]]. ==Preseason friendlies== The 2020 pre-season saw three matches played including a [[Testimonial match|testimonial]], against [[Leeds Rhinos]], for academy graduate [[Liam Farrell]] who has been playing for the first team since the [[2010 Wigan Warriors season|2010 season]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.wiganwarriors.com/news/2020-01-18-liam-farrell-testimonial-preview|title=Liam Farrell Testimonial Preview | News | Wigan Warriors|website=www.wiganwarriors.com}} The first match against the [[London Skolars]] was a continuation of an annual event between the Warriors and Skolars, resulting in a 70–6 win by the away side. The second match against the Leeds Rhinos resulted in a 10–22 loss for Wigan. The final match against derby rivals [[Leigh Centurions]], ended in a 6–12 loss for the Warriors. {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;" !Date !Opponent !H/A !Result !Scorers !Att. !Ref. |- style="background:#ddffdd;" | 17 January 2020 |{{leagueicon|London Skolars|16}} [[London Skolars]] | A | 70–6 | | | |- style="background:#ffdddd;" | 19 January 2020 |{{leagueicon|leeds|16}} [[Leeds Rhinos]] | H | 10–22 | | |{{Cite web|url=https://www.instagram.com/p/B7gigpsFbxn/ |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/iarchive/instagram/wiganwarriorsrl/2224929993288694887 |archive-date=25 December 2021 |url-access=registration|title=Wigan Warriors 🍒⚪ (at 🏡)'s Instagram photo: "FULL TIME | Wigan Warriors 10-22 Leeds Rhinos .
